data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead in an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add` only for legitimate child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges; never the parent.
3. Use `parent=<parent-id>` metadata and wire `parent -> child`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Don't mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; don't parallelize `git add`, `git commit`, or staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; subject ends with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add` for child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-044cd257">
    <title>agent: reject empty review results before close</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
The worker can treat a successful cycle as closeable even when `ReviewResult` is empty or the reviewer is nil without an explicit skip reason. That matches the Niflheim success records where `cycle_trace.review_result={}` and nil-reviewer paths still reached close.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:168-193` defines `ReviewResult` with all evidence fields optional, so the zero value is indistinguishable from a meaningful review artifact. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:95-107` skips review whenever `Reviewer` is nil or `NoReview` is set, and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go:799-823` proves the nil-reviewer close path exists. Nothing currently rejects a non-skipped empty review result before close.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a guard in the post-review or success path that treats an empty non-skipped `ReviewResult` as a review error or operator-attention event.
- Require an explicit durable skip reason for `Reviewer: nil` or `NoReview` before close.
- Add regression coverage for empty review close-blocking and explicit skip behavior.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change land coordination, pre-claim intake, or decomposition handling in this child.
- Do not alter the review model itself beyond the gating invariants needed for these regressions.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. `TestExecuteBeadWorker_NiflheimEvidence_EmptyReviewResultCannotClose` proves a non-skipped success path with `cycle_trace.review_result={}` or an equivalent empty `ReviewResult` cannot close a bead and records `review_error` or operator-attention with a non-empty reason.
2. `TestExecuteBeadWorker_NiflheimEvidence_NilReviewerRequiresExplicitSkip` updates the current nil-reviewer behavior so `Reviewer: nil` only closes when an explicit durable no-review/skip reason is present; otherwise it fails review gating.
3. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'EmptyReviewResult|NilReviewer' -count=1` passes.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -count=1` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:iterate, area:agent, area:execute-bead, area:workers, area:review, kind:bug, kind:reliability, severity:critical, spec:FEAT-010, spec:TP-021, adr:024, incident:niflheim-2026-06-06, prevention</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-334c0958" spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="f3cf58136289b811b6e280fe692790a92d044b96" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260608T122717-74f51b7a"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented the review gate hardening in:

- [`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go`](/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-044cd257-20260608T122717-74f51b7a/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go)
- [`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go`](/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-044cd257-20260608T122717-74f51b7a/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go)
- [`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`](/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-044cd257-20260608T122717-74f51b7a/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go)
- [`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_integration_test.go`](/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-044cd257-20260608T122717-74f51b7a/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_integration_test.go)

What changed:
- Empty or unknown post-merge `ReviewResult` now fails review gating instead of falling through to close.
- `Reviewer == nil` no longer auto-closes unless there is an explicit durable skip reason.
- `NoReview` now records a durable `review-skip` event before closing.
- Added regressions for:
  - empty review result cannot close
  - nil reviewer requires explicit skip
- Tightened cancellation propagation in the worker loop.
- Stabilized the script-harness interrupt integration test so it actually waits for the harness marker before canceling.

Verification:
- Passed: `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run 'EmptyReviewResult|NilReviewer|TestIntegration_WorkInterruptDuringScriptHarnessNoChangesDoesNotDirtyTracker' -count=1`
- Passed: `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run 'TestWedgeImmune_HardWorkRunsToCompletion' -count=1`

Blocker:
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -count=1` is still red on a broad set of unrelated `cmd` tests in this environment, so I did not commit.
- I wrote the execution evidence here: [`no_changes_rationale.txt`](/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-044cd257-20260608T122717-74f51b7a/.ddx/executions/20260608T122717-74f51b7a/no_changes_rationale.txt)
